repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1: finite-grid monotone-concave continuous-treatment sensitivity intervals; no continuous IV, rank-copula tube, or rank-slice slope

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019/FanPark2010: Makarov/coupling bounds for treatment-effect distributions; no continuous-IV rank-slice slope transport envelope

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1: dosewise CMSM shape-pasting, not continuous-IV plus adjacent-dose rank-copula pasting

published_prior_art.cite: Kasy2014/BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025/DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025: marginal continuous-IV or dosewise sensitivity bounds; no one-law cIV+copula pasting theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: binary dynamic IV compliance-memory bounds; no continuous-dose rank-slice slope sign phase

published_prior_art.cite: Kline2016: IV identification of causal-effect direction; no bounded-confounding copula-tube rank-slice local-slope phase transition
FirpoRidder2019: average effect is marginal while distributional functionals are coupling-dependent

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The sign-certification literature comparison should add Kline2016, which is closer on IV-based direction/sign identification than the current Chernozhukov-Hansen and panel-copula comparators alone.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The finite-grid theorem does not define when a rank cell violates the rho-tube; the outer-bound claim needs an explicit representative-cell or any-point cell-admissibility convention.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the finite-grid tube rule explicitly: either ranks live at cell representatives, or a cell is admissible whenever its interval product intersects the rho-tube; for an outer bound, use the latter unless the exact finite law is also representative-valued.
Add Kline2016 to Related work and the Conjecture 2 novelty block as the closest sign/direction-IV comparator, emphasizing that the proposal is partial-ID, continuous-dose, and copula-tube based.
Add a two- or three-bin numerical witness for Conjecture 2 showing positive rho-tube lower slope, nonpositive unrestricted-copula lower slope, and full-population mean interval containing zero.
State the pasting conjecture as a measurable common-latent construction with explicit compatibility conditions between dosewise sensitivity tilts and the selected rank copula.

recommended_next_step: Revise v4 to fix the grid tube convention and add the missed sign-IV literature comparison; no novelty pivot is needed.
